component.name: Heading
component.description: Renders heading-level text using title and display size tokens. The as prop controls semantic HTML level (h1–h6); the size prop controls visual appearance independently. Does not add margin.
component.figmaNodeId: N/A — Heading uses Figma text styles, not a component set. Code Connect requires Enterprise plan.

when: Labelling a page, section, or content region with a heading
Introducing a content block that a Text component will follow

patterns.name: titled-section
patterns.description: A Heading followed by Text inside a Stack to form a titled content block.

patterns.name: hero-block
patterns.description: A display-scale heading centered in a padded Box with supporting Text and an Inline of actions.
patterns.tree: Box(paddingY=xxl) > Stack(gap=md, align=center) > [Heading(size=display, as=h1), Text(size=subheader, color=subtle), Inline(gap=sm) > [Button, Button]]

antiPatterns.scenario: Choosing size to match visual hierarchy instead of semantic level
antiPatterns.reason: size and as are independent — use as for document structure and size for visual scale
antiPatterns.alternative: Set as to the correct heading level (h1 for the page title, h2 for sections, etc.) then choose size independently

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Heading for body copy or labels
antiPatterns.reason: Heading sizes are designed for titles; body copy and labels have their own tokens
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Text with size body-default, body-small, metadata, or label

purpose.subheader: Section sub-labels or feature callouts. 1.375rem semibold.
purpose.headline: Primary section headings. 1.625rem semibold.
purpose.headline-serif: Editorial or feature headings in the serif typeface. 1.75rem medium.
purpose.display: Hero headlines. 2rem serif, medium weight, tight leading.

purpose.default: Primary heading colour on the page surface.
purpose.subtle: De-emphasised heading, e.g. a secondary section label.
purpose.brand: Brand-coloured heading for accent or featured sections.
purpose.inverted: Primary heading colour on an inverted (dark) surface, e.g. a CTA banner.

accessibility.notes: Always set as to the correct heading level for document outline. Size and as are deliberately decoupled so visual scale never forces a skip in heading levels.
